'''Michael McIlorum''' (born 10 January 1988) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. He was both an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] international.

He had played his entire professional career with the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], prior to his move to the South of France.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He won the [[2010 Super League Championship|2010]] and 2013 Super League Championships with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].

==Background==
McIlorum was born 10 January 1988 in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England, and attended [[Farnley Park High School]].

He was a Leeds amateur at Stanningley and Queens. He had been selected for England U16s in 2004 and joined Wigan's under-18s Academy in 2005.

==Playing career==
===Wigan===
McIlorum signed a two-year full-time contract with Wigan in July 2005, keeping him at the club until the end of 2007. McIlorum was selected for Yorkshire U17s after joining Wigan in August 2005. He was also included in the England U17s squad to take on the touring Australian Institute of Sport in 2005. He capped the 2005 season by winning the Wigan Supporters' Association Under 18s Player of the Season award.

McIlorum made his senior début for Wigan on 8 June 2007 in a [[Challenge Cup]] quarter final tie against [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] at the [[JJB Stadium]] after an injury ruled out first team regular [[Shane Millard]].

In 2010 Wigan made the [[2010 Super League Grand Final]], and McIlorum was selected to play as a hooker in the victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Wilson|first=Andy|title=Wigan turn St Helens into sinners with Super League Grand Final win|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/02/wigan-st-helens-grand-final|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=guardian.co.uk|date=2 October 2010}}</ref>

The following year McIlorum played for Wigan from the substitutes' bench in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18–28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18–28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}</ref>

He played in the [[2013 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23794947|title= Hull FC 0–16 Wigan Warriors|date=24 August 2013|work= BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url= https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/aug/24/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final|title= Wigan Warriors overcome Hull FC and elements to win Challenge Cup|date=24 August 2013|work= Guardian|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url= http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3660839,00.html|title= Wigan Warriors grind out victory over Hull|date=24 August 2013|work= Sky Sports|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

Wigan reached the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]], and McIlorum was selected to play at hooker, scoring a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in their victory against the [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] club at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] side at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|publisher=Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}</ref>

In the [[2016 World Club Series]] against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]], McIlorum suffered a serious ankle injury, and did not play again for the rest of the 2016 season.<ref>{{cite news|title=Wigan Warriors: Michael McIlorum to miss rest of year with injury|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/35695873|website=BBC Sport|access-date=13 December 2017|date=1 March 2016}}</ref>

He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18–14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>
[[File:Michael McIlorum.jpg|thumb|right|McIlorum playing for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]]]]

===Catalans Dragons===
In January 2018, McIlorum signed a two-year deal with the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/42547355|title=Michael McIlorum: Catalans Dragons sign Wigan Warriors hooker on a two-year deal |work=BBC Sport|date=2 January 2018|access-date=24 January 2018}}</ref>

He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] club at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|work=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

In the 2020 season, he played in the club's elimination final victory over Leeds but was placed on report during the game.  He was later suspended for six matches after pleading guilty to two charges of a dangerous high tackle and other contrary conduct.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://superleague.co.uk/article/1735/mcilorum-banned-for-six-matches|title=McIlorum banned for six matches|website=www.superleague.co.uk}}</ref>

On 9 October 2021, he played for Catalans in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10–12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, McIlorum played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

On 25 September 2025, it was announced that McIlorum would return to the Dragons as an assistant Coach, alongside former teammate [[Mitchell Pearce|Mitchell Pierce]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dragons Catalans › News › 2025 › Sept. › New assistant coaches appointed |url=http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/articles-9/302-12963-new-assistant-coaches-appointed/ |access-date=2025-09-22 |website=Les Dragons Catalans}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 18 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for Hull Kingston Rovers in the Super League on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=18 Jul 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|author=Aaron Bower|title=Michael McIlorum explains reasons behind Hull KR move after agreeing shock deal for 2025|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/michael-mcilorum-explains-reasons-behind-hull-kr-move-after-agreeing-shock-deal-for-2025}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, he played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 18 September 2025, Hull Kingston Rovers won the League Leaders Shield in victory over Warrington, McIlorum played a part in Hull Kingston Rovers 1st place season. <ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo</ref>
On 25 September 2025, it was reported that he had decided to retire.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=25 Sep 2025|accessdate=26 Sep 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Hull KR boss Willie Peters pays tribute to Michael McIlorum following retirement decision|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-kr-boss-willie-peters-pays-tribute-to-michael-mcilorum-following-retirement-decision/}}</ref>
On 9 October 2025, he played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
===Ireland===
Being from Irish heritage, McIlorum was named in the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Cassidy included in ireland squad">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in ireland squad|work=BBC Sport|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref>

After not being selected to play for England again after the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]], McIlorum changed his international allegiance back to Ireland in 2017 and was named in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]] squad.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===England===
In 2012 McIlorum changed his international allegiance to England and made his England début in the 2012 Autumn International Series against Wales playing at hooker. The following year he was selected as part of [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s squad for their [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]] campaign.

Nine years after his last Test cap for England, he was named in the team's 24-man squad for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web |title=England Men's Rugby League World Cup Squad Revealed |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60998/england-mens-rugby-league-world-cup-squad-revealed- |publisher=The Rugby Football League |access-date=11 October 2022 |date=30 September 2022}}</ref>
